title: Skip frontier evaluations when feedback window has no rows
description: Implement the no-feedback story by tightening feedback-window dataset reuse and marking variants as no_data when no feedback-derived rows exist. This replaces the earlier suspected explicit-sample handoff; user clarified the score has no feedback samples, so no evaluations should be dispatched for that window.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Observed frontier feedback evaluations invoking evaluate feedback --version. That command injects a FeedbackItems data section into temporary score YAML but constructs AccuracyEvaluation without labeled_samples, making it depend on AccuracyEvaluation fallback YAML loading. For TactusScore/no-data-section cases the fallback can fail and surfaces as Dataset not found. Next: add a failing unit around the explicit sample handoff, then patch feedback evaluation to pass samples directly.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: New user clarification: this score has no feedback samples. The immediate bug is not the TactusScore config path; the frontier procedure dispatches feedback-backed evaluations even when the feedback-derived sample set is empty. Adjusting implementation target: detect zero feedback-derived rows and emit no_data rows instead of failing the whole procedure. The explicit labeled_samples handoff may still be useful later but is not required for this fix.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented: model_performance_frontier now requires a feedback_target_hash before reusing associated regression datasets, builds a fresh feedback-window dataset otherwise, skips regression unless enough rows exist, skips feedback when the feedback-window row count is zero, and marks rows no_data through build_result_row. Tests added for no_data row status and procedure contract strings. Focused tests and YAML parse passed.
